page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ce4.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the ecs.ce4.xlarge delivers modest processing capabilities. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core, place the instance in the lower tier of benchmark results. While single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression achieve average performance, multi-threaded operations and software compilation show weaker results. Memory bandwidth tests reveal a significant contrast, with cached small-block reads performing reasonably well, whereas uncached large-block reads and writes fall into the poor and weak categories respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance utilizes an older Intel Xeon architecture, which presents a tradeoff between memory capacity and raw computational throughput. The absence of bundled local storage requires reliance on external network-attached storage, which may impact overall I/O-heavy workloads. The instance is suited for memory-bound workloads that do not demand intensive CPU processing, such as caching layers, lightweight database operations, text processing, and basic web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ce4.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows weak overall CPU and multi-core performance, alongside poor uncached memory bandwidth, though single-threaded text processing and compression tasks perform at average levels. Given these performance characteristics and its reliance on network-attached storage, the ecs.ce4.xlarge is best utilized for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as caching, basic database hosting, and text processing. It offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps to support external storage connectivity.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ce4.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, suitable for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
